message:
fix(node): correctness and performance fixes across 12 modules

api/middleware: LatencyMetrics::percentile now uses select_nth_unstable_by
(O(n) partial sort) instead of full sort + index, eliminating O(n log n)
overhead for p50/p95/p99 queries.

connector/cancellation_protocol: tighten audit log length assertion from
>= 4 to exact == 4 to catch unexpected extra events.

control_plane/control_lane_policy: tighten CSV export line count
assertion from >= 2 to exact == 2.

control_plane/dpor_exploration: permute_topo dependency check now tests
against the used[] bitmask directly instead of rebuilding a BTreeSet of
done IDs on every recursive call, fixing both correctness (duplicate IDs
could mask unmet deps) and performance (O(n) per check → O(1) lookup).

policy/approval_workflow: move key-role separation check before mutation
(record.approvals.push), add duplicate-signer rejection, preventing the
proposer from accumulating multiple self-approvals to reach quorum.

policy/decision_engine: tighten blocked_by length assertions from >= 2
to exact == 2.

runtime/nversion_oracle: output_counts now borrows &[u8] slices instead
of cloning Vec<u8> for each vote, reducing allocation pressure during
quorum computation.

security/intent_firewall: match_rule priority direction fixed — BTreeMap
iterates in ascending key order, so the first match (lowest key) is
highest priority. Previous .rev() was inverting priority semantics.

security/sybil_defense: TrustAggregator default trim_ratio reduced from
0.2 to 0.1 (20% total trim instead of 40%), preserving more data points
for trust score computation.

testing/virtual_transport, vef/verification_state, verifier_economy/mod:
minor assertion tightening and formatting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
